rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-aryl methacrylamides using an N-triflyl benzimidazolium salt as the CF3 radical source to form CF3-bearing oxindoles/isoquinolinediones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation generates the trifluoromethyl radical from the N-triflyl benzimidazolium reagent (1); the C-centered radical formed after CF3 addition to the acrylamide undergoes further anodic oxidation to a cation that is trapped by intramolecular arene cyclization

key_intermediates.name: trifluoromethyl radical (CF3•) released from N-triflyl benzimidazolium salt
alkyl radical from CF3 addition to methacrylamide alkene
benzylic/alkyl cation after anodic oxidation of alkyl radical
benzimidazole byproduct from reagent 1 after CF3 loss

electron_flow: At the anode, the N-triflyl benzimidazolium cation (1) is oxidized/fragmented to release a CF3 radical, which adds to the alkene of the methacrylamide; the resulting carbon radical is further oxidized at the anode to a cation that undergoes intramolecular Friedel–Crafts-type cyclization onto the N-aryl ring, followed by rearomatization to give the CF3-substituted heterocycle.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 5a formed, indicating radical intermediate involvement

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 5a trace; CF3-DPE adduct detected by HRMS ([M]+ found 248.0807, calc 248.0812), confirming CF3 radical

probe_reagents.name: CF3SO3Na (sodium triflinate/triflate) as alternative CF3 source
probe_reagents.observation: No 5a obtained, indicating CF3 radical does not arise from triflate fragment

probe_reagents.name: Benzimidazolium BF4 salt 1b (instead of OTf counterion)
probe_reagents.observation: 5a obtained in 63% yield, confirming that the CF3 radical originates from the N-triflyl benzimidazolium cation portion of reagent 1

notes: Counterion-swap experiment (OTf- vs BF4-) combined with CF3SO3Na control elegantly localizes the CF3 source to the N-Tf group on the benzimidazolium cation rather than the triflate counterion. Solvent screen identifies MeCN as optimal (44%); concentration has little effect (42-44% over 0.025-0.1 M). Subsequent anodizing verification experiment (section 3.3) likely supports anodic initiation but details not shown on these pages.
